definition: An ordinary person not yet transformed by noble-path breakthrough.
notes: The project prefers ordinary person because it is contemporary and avoids the dated sound of worldling. Worldling remains acceptable in explanatory prose when the contrast with ariyasāvaka needs the familiar technical idiom.

context_rules.context: In most doctrinal and comparative contexts
In explanatory prose using established Buddhist technical idiom

example_phrases.notes: Frequent canonical contrast to the noble disciple.
